title: Stop Or My Dog Will Shoot

jokes.text: Pearls are not oyster barf
jokes.setup: Classic chalkboard gag opening - Bart writing lines as punishment
jokes.punchline: Absurd correction of a crude misconception about pearl formation
jokes.explanation: Strong opening gag with perfect economy. The juxtaposition of scientific accuracy with juvenile language is quintessentially Simpsons.

jokes.text: Sorry, sir, this is a harvest festival. No alcohol. I'm gonna have to confiscate those steins.
jokes.setup: Homer excitedly expects Oktoberfest with beer
jokes.punchline: It's actually an alcohol-free harvest festival
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer disappointment. Predictable but well-executed character comedy.

jokes.text: Wagon wheels were the Internet of the 19th century! Really? No.
jokes.setup: Pompous historical comparison to make wagon-making sound important
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation with casual 'No'
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure. The absurd historical comparison followed by deadpan rejection is expertly timed and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Styrofoam? You stuffed your horn?! What? Everybody does it.
jokes.setup: Impressive-looking cornucopia display
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be fake, with casual admission of widespread cheating
jokes.explanation: Good visual reveal joke with Homer's typical outrage at deception, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: I can testify at the trial. Ain't gon' be no trial.
jokes.setup: Someone offers to testify, suggesting legal proceedings
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal implying vigilante justice
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good economy. The contrast between civic duty and implied lawlessness works well.

jokes.text: Where the hell is Abigail Adams?! Oh, there she is. Where the hell is Mary Todd Lincoln?! Oh, she's right there. Where the hell is Ida Saxton McKinley?!
jokes.setup: Homer getting increasingly agitated about missing first ladies
jokes.punchline: Escalates from famous to obscure presidents' wives, showing obsessive knowledge
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke. Homer's unexpected expertise in obscure first ladies is perfect character comedy - he knows trivia but misses obvious things.

jokes.text: Puh! I didn't come here to be insulted. We're leaving!
jokes.setup: Corn maze sign says 'Betcha can't solve our maze'
jokes.punchline: Homer takes generic challenge as personal insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - taking innocent marketing as personal affront. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Sure is a lot of corn in this parking lot. Dad, we're in the maze.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks they're still in parking lot
jokes.punchline: They're already lost in the maze without realizing it
jokes.explanation: Good setup for maze sequence. Homer's obliviousness is in character and economically established.

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes they're lost
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase. Zero originality but perfect character integration and maximum economy.

jokes.text: Corn. More corn. Another kid getting thrown in the air. Witch's coven. Seattle Space Needle. Amateur production of You Can't Take It With You
jokes.setup: Bart surveys the maze from above
jokes.punchline: List escalates from mundane to increasingly bizarre sightings
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from normal to surreal. The specificity of 'You Can't Take It With You' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Split up?! Marge, no! We can fix this marriage!
jokes.setup: Marge suggests splitting up to solve the maze
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks she means divorce
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke. Homer's leap to marital crisis from innocent suggestion is beautifully in character.

jokes.text: Before I met you, I had friends and dreams! Oh, please take me back. The dating scene is a nightmare. I'm begging you!
jokes.setup: Homer's dramatic declaration of independence
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal to desperate pleading
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's emotional volatility and dependence on Marge.

jokes.text: Of our marriage?! I don't want to live! I don't want to live...!
jokes.setup: Marge mentions finding 'a way out'
jokes.punchline: Homer again misinterprets as divorce talk and becomes suicidal
jokes.explanation: Callback to previous misunderstanding but with less impact. Repetitive but maintains character consistency.

jokes.text: Hey, this maze is made of corn!
jokes.setup: Homer has been lost in corn maze for extended time
jokes.punchline: Only now realizes the obvious fact about its construction
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness. Simple but effective character moment showing his delayed processing.

jokes.text: Electrified for your enjoyment. Signs also electrified.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to break through maze wall, gets shocked
jokes.punchline: Even the warning signs are electrified
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of the electrified maze gag. The meta-joke of electrified warning signs is well-crafted.

jokes.text: How come things that happen to stupid people keep happening to me?
jokes.setup: Homer gets repeatedly shocked by electrified maze
jokes.punchline: Complete lack of self-awareness about why 'stupid' things happen to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer line. The complete absence of self-reflection while asking a profound question is quintessential character comedy.

jokes.text: No, you didn't. Your plan was to burn the corn. That was never my plan.
jokes.setup: Homer claims credit for solving maze with algorithm
jokes.punchline: Lisa deflates his claim, Homer immediately denies his actual plan
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay. Homer's instant revisionist history is believable and mildly funny.

jokes.text: I don't have any holders!
jokes.setup: Told to eat corn while lost and starving
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre priority of needing corn holders
jokes.explanation: Absurd Homer logic. In a survival situation, he's concerned about dining etiquette - perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: We'll send a member of our elite canine unit in to find your husband's corpse. Uh, living corpse.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum offers police help
jokes.punchline: Assumes Homer is dead, awkwardly corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence. The casual assumption of death followed by awkward correction fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Chief, I'm afraid Officer Jaws is guilty of littering... and here's the litter!
jokes.setup: Lou reports on police dog's performance
jokes.punchline: Dog had puppies during duty
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay joke. 'Litter' works on two levels - garbage and baby animals. Clean setup and payoff.

jokes.text: He just needs it for walking upstairs.
jokes.setup: Sports bra found in Homer's backpack
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact explanation of Homer's physical needs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant uncomfortable moment. Marge's casual acceptance of Homer's embarrassing reality is both touching and hilarious.

jokes.text: In case I don't make it, here are my Oscar picks for next year. Best Sound Effects Editing: the team from...
jokes.setup: Homer writing what appears to be final words
jokes.punchline: Instead of heartfelt goodbye, he records Oscar predictions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer absurdity. In his 'final moments' he focuses on trivial entertainment awards rather than family - brilliant character writing.

jokes.text: I don't think he's ever thought about anything.
jokes.setup: Asked if Santa's Little Helper has considered law enforcement career
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt assessment of the dog's mental capacity
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. Homer's honest assessment cuts through the anthropomorphizing of the dog.

jokes.text: No, and never bring it up again! Please, can he? All right.
jokes.setup: Bart asks if dog can be police officer
jokes.punchline: Homer's instant reversal from absolute no to agreement
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer weakness under pressure. His complete capitulation is predictable but in character.

jokes.text: For the next two weeks, I'm gonna be your worst enemy, except this Thursday is the Fourth of July, so we'll take Friday off as well. Well, I guess Wednesday everybody's going to be thinking about the long weekend, so...eh, what the hell? Why don't we just take the whole week off?
jokes.setup: Tough police trainer speech about rigorous training
jokes.punchline: Gradually talks himself out of any actual training
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke. The logical progression from one holiday to complete work avoidance is beautifully structured.

jokes.text: Why did you let me do that? We are so far behind.
jokes.setup: Trainer realizes he just cancelled all training
jokes.punchline: Blames his audience for his own poor decision-making
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to previous joke. Blaming others for his own weakness adds another layer of character comedy.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa. 'Blow'? 'Cage'? You're in uniform, Lou. Don't slang it up.
jokes.setup: Lou uses police slang terms
jokes.punchline: Wiggum objects to professional terminology while being unprofessional himself
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum moment. His misguided attempt at professionalism while being unprofessional is classic character comedy.

jokes.text: Keep your noses clean and your hearts free of worms.
jokes.setup: Formal police graduation ceremony
jokes.punchline: Wiggum mixes metaphorical advice with literal dog health concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum confusion between human and canine advice. The mixture of metaphor and literalism is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: It ain't like Air Bud V: Strong Arm Of The Paw.
jokes.setup: Lou gives rookie dog advice about street life
jokes.punchline: References fictional sequel to kids' movie series
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference. The made-up sequel title fits the Air Bud pattern but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: You gave that hog a day of beauty, and I didn't get nothing! Manicure... pedicure... exfoliation!
jokes.setup: Domestic dispute between rural couple
jokes.punchline: Wife jealous that pig got spa treatment she didn't
jokes.explanation: Standard hillbilly stereotype humor. The pig spa treatment is mildly absurd but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Is you man or turtle? Here's your answer. Skittle, skittle, scurry, skittle.
jokes.setup: Wife challenges Cletus's masculinity
jokes.punchline: Cletus literally acts like a turtle, making turtle sounds
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment. Cletus's literal interpretation and turtle performance is appropriately absurd for the character.

jokes.text: I even have to eat my own homework.
jokes.setup: Bart misses his dog
jokes.punchline: Reversal of 'dog ate my homework' excuse
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of classic excuse. Perfect economy and good wordplay that makes emotional sense for the character.

jokes.text: Chief, why are you using the walkie-talkie? I can hear you fine. Fine. Let's not use any of our police equipment.
jokes.setup: Wiggum uses radio unnecessarily
jokes.punchline: Defensive overreaction to logical criticism
jokes.explanation: Standard Wiggum incompetence. His defensive reaction is in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I want to pump my guns. I want to rip my pecs. I want to shriv my nards.
jokes.setup: Man wants steroids for muscle building
jokes.punchline: Escalates to nonsense gym terminology ending in 'nards'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with made-up gym terminology. 'Shriv my nards' is appropriately ridiculous conclusion.

jokes.text: Nobody ever freezes. Might as well just say 'run.'
jokes.setup: Police shout 'Freeze!' at suspect
jokes.punchline: Lou's practical observation about ineffective police commands
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor. Lou's practical analysis of police procedure futility is both funny and surprisingly insightful.

jokes.text: Guilty... of being innocent!
jokes.setup: Judge asks how defendant pleads
jokes.punchline: Paradoxical plea that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels somewhat forced. The paradox is clever but doesn't have strong character motivation.

jokes.text: Don't worry, dude, I'm going straight... straight to my customers to sell more drugs.
jokes.setup: Drug dealer appears to reform
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's continuing criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection structure. Well-executed but fairly predictable given the setup.

jokes.text: Now that he's had a taste of human flesh, the dog will be a slave to its succulence. Look at him eyeing me. He knows I taste best.
jokes.setup: Dog bit Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid overreaction and narcissistic assumption he tastes best
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer narcissism. Even in paranoia about being eaten, he assumes he's the most delicious option available.

jokes.text: Me, I knit violent imagery. Pretty grim, huh?
jokes.setup: Lou explains how cops handle stress
jokes.punchline: Shows knitted scene of violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of domestic craft with violent imagery. The contrast between knitting and violence is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: What if Santa's Little Helper stops being a police dog, then he can get a nonviolent job like barking songs on novelty Christmas records.
jokes.setup: Bart suggests alternative career for the dog
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and completely impractical suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good Bart absurdity. The specificity of 'novelty Christmas records' shows his child-like solution thinking.

jokes.text: No, boy, that's Hanukah! Forget it, kid. He's a cop now.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to get dog to bark Christmas songs
jokes.punchline: Dog barks wrong holiday song, Lou dismisses training attempt
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about dog training confusion and religious mix-up

jokes.text: Oh, relax, Marge. I'm just messing with you. I know I'm not a dog. I'm a people, like you.
jokes.setup: Homer appears to think he's a dog
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was joking, but uses wrong grammar proving his intelligence level
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment. The grammatical error 'I'm a people' undermines his attempt to appear intelligent while joking.

jokes.text: They don't love us. They just love the uniform.
jokes.setup: Woman excited to meet police dog
jokes.punchline: Lou's cynical observation about police relationships
jokes.explanation: Good character insight. Lou's cynicism about public perception of police adds depth while being humorous.

jokes.text: We'll get you their most vicious street cur! He'll bite your ass raw!
jokes.setup: Bart wants a mean dog
jokes.punchline: Employee enthusiastically offers violent dog with crude language
jokes.explanation: Somewhat crude humor without much craft. The escalation works but relies on shock value rather than cleverness.

jokes.text: Our pythons cost five dollars a foot. We're not made of money!
jokes.setup: Employee quotes snake prices by length
jokes.punchline: Homer's budget consciousness about python purchase
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer cheapness joke. The pricing structure is mildly absurd but the reaction is predictable.

jokes.text: It says here don't bother naming them. Snakes have poor hearing, and only live to strangle. I like it. 'Strangles.'
jokes.setup: Care instructions say don't name snakes
jokes.punchline: Bart names it based on its murderous nature
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor for Bart. His excitement about the snake's violent nature fits his mischievous character perfectly.

jokes.text: He's not slimy at all. He's scaly. Eww! He is slimy! That's because I soaked him in slime!
jokes.setup: Bart corrects Lisa about snake texture, she touches and confirms sliminess
jokes.punchline: Reveals he deliberately made it slimy to trick her
jokes.explanation: Good sibling prank setup. Bart's premeditated grossness toward Lisa is classic brother behavior with solid structure.

jokes.text: He's not slimy at all. He's scaly. Eww! He is slimy! That's because I soaked him in slime!
jokes.setup: Bart reassuring Lisa that snake isn't slimy
jokes.punchline: Reveals he deliberately made it slimy to trick her
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and perfect Bart sibling prank behavior

jokes.text: Well, I can breathe out okay.
jokes.setup: Marge asks if Homer can breathe while being strangled by snake
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal and oddly optimistic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic under pressure. His literal interpretation of breathing while being strangled is absurdly optimistic and economically delivered.

jokes.text: Don't... give... snake... attention. It's what... he really... wants!
jokes.setup: Homer being strangled by snake
jokes.punchline: Homer treating strangulation as attention-seeking behavior
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating life-threatening situation as behavioral issue

jokes.text: After months of practice, I have trained my rabbit to hop in perfect tempo to Mussorgsky's immortal Pictures at an Exhibition.
jokes.setup: Pet talent show participant describes their act
jokes.punchline: Incredibly pretentious description of basic rabbit hopping
jokes.explanation: Good pretentious character comedy. The contrast between classical music and simple animal behavior is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Hey, don't ask me to show if you can't handle the tell.
jokes.setup: Principal complains about snake at show and tell
jokes.punchline: Bart's reversal of 'show and tell' into defensive comeback
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay from Bart. The reversal of a familiar phrase into a defensive retort is well-crafted and in character.

jokes.text: Mine was as funny as his. No one's arguing with you, lady.
jokes.setup: Female teacher insists her comment was equally funny
jokes.punchline: Male teacher's blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment. The dismissive response is economical but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: I taste the flavor. It's a very mild mint. Well, maybe, because it's a minty color, your mind is fooling your tongue. I know what I taste!
jokes.setup: Discussion about Shamrock Shake flavoring
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's stubborn insistence despite logical explanation
jokes.explanation: Standard Wiggum stubbornness. His refusal to accept logic is in character but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: Children, line up according to height. No, weight. No, the sum total of the letters in your last name if each letter is assigned a numeric value according to its place in the alphabet. Quickly, now!
jokes.setup: Teacher giving evacuation instructions
jokes.punchline: Instructions become increasingly complex and impractical
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke. The progression from simple to mathematically complex organization during emergency is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: He's not following his nose this time. He's following his heart. Sometimes I forget why you're chief, but I remember now!
jokes.setup: Wiggum makes surprisingly poetic observation about the dog
jokes.punchline: Lou's surprised recognition of Wiggum's rare insight
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment. Wiggum's rare moment of wisdom followed by Lou's genuine appreciation works well for both characters.

jokes.text: Oh, man, it's like some chemicals cut one.
jokes.setup: Bart encounters toxic gas in school
jokes.punchline: Compares dangerous chemicals to flatulence
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor appropriate for Bart but not particularly clever. Relies on crude comparison for laughs.

jokes.text: Sorry, but a boy's got to go with his dog. If he hurts you again, don't call me.
jokes.setup: Snake chooses to stay with Bart over Homer
jokes.punchline: Snake gives relationship advice like a jealous friend
jokes.explanation: Good anthropomorphization of the snake. Treating it like a protective friend giving relationship advice is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: Who am I kidding, I'll always be there for you.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming he won't help if dog hurts him again
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits his unconditional love
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Bart's genuine attachment despite tough talk

jokes.text: That'll do, snake. That'll do.
jokes.setup: Snake helped in rescue
jokes.punchline: Homer uses 'Babe' movie catchphrase for snake
jokes.explanation: Simple movie reference callback. Works as a conclusion but doesn't add much originality or insight.
